statement: A greenfield rewrite can delete scheduler_locks, llm_jobs, llm_batches, and process_request_targets, while keeping domain tables and optionally retaining a slim append-only LLM audit ledger.
rationale: Those tables currently exist to emulate a workflow runtime rather than represent business truth, while domain artifacts are independently meaningful.
keyAssumptions: Temporal becomes the only runtime owner for new executions
Auditability can be satisfied by a simpler attempt ledger or by Temporal history plus Axiom
validationCriteria: The rewritten engine can run without DB-backed lease tables or transport queues
Scientific outputs remain reproducible and inspectable after the table reduction
critiquePoints: Removing llm_requests entirely may hurt auditability and artifact traceability
questions: Should prompt bodies and raw outputs live in Convex, object storage, or only Axiom/Temporal history?
